main.title: Why Do You Procrastinate?
main.description:
You want to start. You fully intend to. And you scrub the kitchen instead, feeling terrible the whole time. That's not laziness, and what it actually is has puzzled people for thousands of years.

What you'll learn:
- The giveaway that procrastination is almost the OPPOSITE of laziness
- What you're really avoiding (it isn't the work)
- The ancient Greek word for knowing better and doing it anyway
- The most backwards-sounding fix in psychology, from a real study

What are you procrastinating on right now, watching this? Drop it below.

SOURCES
- Procrastination as short-term mood repair, not time management (Sirois & Pychyl 2013).
- Akrasia, debated by Socrates and Aristotle (Routledge Encyclopedia of Philosophy).
- Etymology: Latin pro + cras, "to put off until tomorrow" (Etymonline).
- Self-forgiveness reduces future procrastination (Wohl, Pychyl & Bennett 2010).

This video uses AI-assisted narration and illustrations.

#psychology #procrastination #yourbrain #motivation #wonder
